question: Create a multi-level inheritance hierarchy: `Animal` (with `name`), `Mammal` (derives from `Animal`, adds `furColor`), and `Dog` (derives from `Mammal`, adds `breed`). Each class should have a constructor and a `printDetails()` method.

test_cases.expected:
Name: Buddy, Fur Color: Golden, Breed: Golden Retriever
Dog destructor: Buddy, Breed: Golden Retriever
Mammal destructor: Buddy, Color: Golden
Animal destructor: Buddy

--- Demonstrating Robustness Issue (Non-virtual destructor) ---
Name: Max, Fur Color: Black, Breed: Labrador
Deleting polymorphicAnimal...
Animal destructor: Max
PolymorphicAnimal deleted. (Note: Only Animal destructor was called for Max)

errors: Non-virtual destructor in the base class 'Animal' leads to undefined behavior and potential resource leaks when derived objects are deleted via base class pointers (object slicing during destruction).
